title: Lab: Repair an Assistance Plan Comparison
summary: Lab: Repair an Assistance Plan Comparison retrieves prior skills in an original unfamiliar flexible sizing and source-order incident scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply flex formatting context, flex sizing algorithm, flex alignment, flex wrapping, flex order accessibility, flex pattern selection in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real unfamiliar flexible sizing and source-order incident artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey one-dimensional layout work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the resilient Flexbox patterns here. */

options.text: Unfamiliar flexible sizing and source-order incident case 1: use justify-content for every horizontal decision because horizontal always means main axis.
Unfamiliar flexible sizing and source-order incident case 1: read writing mode, direction, and flex-direction first, then name the main and cross axes before choosing sizing or alignment.
Unfamiliar flexible sizing and source-order incident case 1: apply flex properties to nested descendants because every descendant is a flex item.

options.text: Unfamiliar flexible sizing and source-order incident case 3: flex one means each item always receives exactly the same width.

options.text: Unfamiliar flexible sizing and source-order incident case 12: flexbox is the modern replacement for every older layout mechanism.
